channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Fowler's Manchester City wins WSL title ahead of Matildas teammates
item.description: Australian star Mary Fowler becomes an English football champion with Manchester City after Arsenal blow their WSL title hopes with a draw at Brighton.

media:group.media:description: Mary Fowler's Manchester City are WSL champions for the first time in 10 years.

item.title: Victorians wake to snow in May month out from ski season opening
item.description: Snow has fallen on Victoria's higher peaks overnight, as below-zero temperatures blast the state.
item.dc:creator: Mim Hook and Lily Kristanto

media:group.media:description: Motorists are being advised to take extra care on icy roads this morning.

item.title: Commercial fisher raises $25k to fight demersal fishing ban in WA
item.description: The owners of a Bunbury seafood business raise money to fund a legal challenge against the WA government's controversial demersal fishing ban.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Becerra Mellet

media:group.media:description: Commercial fisherman and seafood retailer Brian Scimone is planning to fight the fishing ban.

item.title: PSG see off Bayern in gripping Champions League semi to set Arsenal date
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain will face Arsenal in the Champions League final after a 1-1 second-leg draw at Bayern Munich sealed the reigning champions' passage through.

media:group.media:description: Paris Saint-Germain showcased their defensive solidity to see off Bayern Munich.

item.title: Push to double Labor's $10 billion housing fund as budget looms
item.description: Australia's peak body for homelessness is leading calls for the federal government to pour at least $10 billion extra into social and affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Jeuniewic

media:group.media:description: Homelessness Australia chief executive Kate Colvin says demand for social housing is rising.

item.title: Aussie track stars nipping at each other's heels and racing the clock
item.description: Eddie Nketia is on the other side of the world, but his times have him right alongside the best sprinters Australia has seen, and he has lofty goals for his future and the country.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Eddie Nketia switched allegiances from New Zealand to Australia late last year.

item.title: Ballard to lead Para squad at Glasgow — and into Games record books
item.description: When it comes to Australian Para sporting legends, few names match it with Angie Ballard, who will lead the Para athletics contingent to a historic fifth Commonwealth Games at Glasgow in July.
item.dc:creator: Damien Peck

media:group.media:description: Australian Para athlete Angie Ballard will return to the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow for an historic fifth time.

item.title: Exmouth residents displaced as town recovers from Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Exmouth was already part of the national housing shortage, but since Cyclone Narelle, the problem has reached a crisis point.
item.dc:creator: Kimberley Putland and Kelly Gudgeon

media:group.media:description: Tahila Paul stands in the house she had finished renovating one week before Narelle hit.

item.title: Senior classes arrive in remote outback town after 150 years
item.description: For more than a century, outback students have had to leave home to finish school. Now, a remote Queensland school is changing that.
item.dc:creator: Tim Little

media:group.media:description: Blake Gregory is studying a certificate II while training for the Oceania Championships.

item.title: Cummins clips centurion Connolly as Aussies light up IPL
item.description: Man of the match Pat Cummins inspires Hyderabad to victory, while also enjoying a young Aussie opponent hitting a century.

media:group.media:description: Pat Cummins was man of the match in Hyderabad Sunrisers' win over Punjab Kings.

item.title: Housing and cost-of-living relief at heart of WA budget
item.description: Housing, health care and cost-of-living relief are likely to feature prominently in Western Australia's budget as more people struggle.
item.dc:creator: Cason Ho

media:group.media:description: Affordable housing for Western Australians is a priority in this year's state budget.

item.title: Mother's Day gathering breaks 'invisible silence' of baby loss
item.description: High tea events around the country are helping women of all ages and cultures feel connected and heard as they grieve their pregnancy and infant loss.
item.dc:creator: Emma Siossian

media:group.media:description: A bereaved mother is offered support at Frances Perry House maternity hospital in Melbourne.

item.title: Live: ASX rebound set to continue as Wall Street's record rally rolls on
item.description: Renewed hopes for Middle East peace drive Wall Street higher and oil down, with the ASX expected to rise again in response. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Letts

media:group.media:description: Traders work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange.

item.title: Live: 'That's assault': Liberal senator scuffles with One Nation volunteer at polling booth
item.description: James Paterson has been involved in a scuffle with a One Nation volunteer at a polling booth in Farrer just days ahead of voters deciding who will represent the regional NSW seat. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Liberal senator James Paterson has clashed with a One National volunteer in the Seat of Farrer.

item.title: Victoria's treasurer says she has 'a lot of sleepless nights'
item.description: The state budget landed on the same day the Reserve Bank once again jacked up interest rates. ABC Melbourne listeners say the cost of living is keeping them awake at night.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Epstein

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jaclyn Symes admits she wishes Victoria's debt were lower.

item.title: Farmers wary of new locust outbreak, despite forecast El Niño
item.description: Swarms of locusts in South Australia are not likely to reach outbreak levels, according to experts, with potential El Niño weather conditions forecast.
item.dc:creator: Jackson Byrne

media:group.media:description: Locusts are everywhere in paddocks across South Australia's Riverland.

item.title: Kids throwing rocks at an Alice Springs riot should be a wake-up call to all of us
item.description: The riot in Alice Springs was yet another flashpoint for the small town trying to heal from heartbreak. So what does the future hold for Central Australia?
item.dc:creator: Matt Garrick

media:group.media:description: Children could be seen taking part in rioting behaviour during the unrest seen outside Alice Springs hospital last week.

item.title: Nikita Tzsyu maintains incredible family record as world title bid looms
item.description: Australian boxing star Nikita Tszyu preserves his unbeaten record with a sixth-round stoppage of Spaniard Oscar Diaz in their super-welterweight showdown.

media:group.media:description: Nikita Tszyu celebrates victory against Oscar Diaz after their super welterweight title bout.

item.title: NSW gun industry calls for buyback to be scrapped amid plummeting sales
item.description: Gun sales are plummeting across NSW, with the industry calling for a proposed buyback to be scrapped. But a public health expert says safety must take priority over retail concerns.
item.dc:creator: David Claughton and Lauren Bohane

media:group.media:description: NSW gun shops say business has dropped since new gun laws were passed.

item.title: How the Tokyo Metro became the terrifying setting for film Exit 8
item.description: The creator of the minimalist horror film hopes it inspires viewers to stop doomscrolling and take a look at the world around them.
item.dc:creator: Gianfranco Di Giovanni

media:group.media:description: Set almost entirely within a single hallway Exit 8 copies the layout of the groundbreaking video game.

item.title: 'Plenty of fish' but algae bloom 'uncertainty' persists at SA tourist town
item.description: The damage caused by South Australia's harmful algal bloom is continuing to be documented on the Yorke Peninsula, even as government water testing shows reduced levels of karenia across most of the coastline.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Merritt

media:group.media:description: Marine biologist Stefan Andrews has been documenting environmental changes since elevated levels of the algae bloom were first detected at Port Hughes in late March.

item.title: Paddock-to-plate revival as farmers grapple with surging food freight costs
item.description: As the cost of each kilometre food travels explodes, growers and consumers are looking for ways to shorten the supply chain.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson and Tayla Larsen

media:group.media:description: More consumers are looking local when it comes to buying fresh produce.

item.title: Live: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since Hezbollah ceasefire, Iran weighs up a peace deal
item.description: The strikes come as Iran weighs up a peace deal put forward by the US, with Donald Trump saying the war could "be at an end". Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Israel has carried out strikes on Beirut for the first time in weeks.

item.title: 'I'll show no mercy': Charity founder faces 'tickle torture' allegations
item.description: More victims of former Order of Australia recipient Cody Schaeffer have spoken to the ABC about his actions.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie and Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: Cody Schaeffer pleaded guilty in March to assaulting five teenagers at his home between 2021 and 2023.

item.title: Kids more likely to be put in hospital by asthma in Melbourne pollution 'hotspot'
item.description: There are renewed calls for government action after another study finds children in Melbourne's inner west are over-represented in asthma-related hospital visits.
item.dc:creator: Leanne Wong

media:group.media:description: Yarraville boy Michael Barbadonis holds an asthma inhaler.
